title: podman machine hangs when M1 Macbook wakes from sleep
body:
### Issue Description

I found this closed and locked bug that describes the issue I am having: #19934.

When my M1 Macbook wakes from sleep, the podman machine is in a hang state. Running any podman command including `podman version` hangs for a long time. I usually ctrl-c to unstick it.

To resolve the issue, I have been running this one-liner, which often returns an error even though the machine seems to work normally after:
```
~/development ❯ podman machine stop ; sleep 3 ; podman machine start
Waiting for VM to exit...
Machine "podman-machine-default" stopped successfully
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: machine did not transition into running state

returned 125 & took 37s
~/development ❯ podman version                                                                        37s
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.9.0
API Version:  4.9.0
Go Version:   go1.21.6
Git Commit:   f7c7b0a7e437b6d4849a9fb48e0e779c3100e337
Built:        Mon Jan 22 15:02:17 2024
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.8.3
API Version:  4.8.3
Go Version:   go1.21.5
Built:        Wed Jan  3 07:10:40 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create podman machine via CLI
2. Close macbook lid overnight
3. Open macbook, and run `podman version` to see it hang


### Describe the results you received

Podman hangs

### Describe the results you expected

Podman should not hang when macbook wakes from sleep, OR at minimum, podman should throw an error quickly if the machine is not in a usable state.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Macbook M1 (ARM)

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
Always nice to cross paths, @BlaineEXE 👋 

Can you try updating Podman to the latest v4.9.2 and try again?  There was a number of fixes that went in.

I destroyed my VM yesterday, brew updated podman, and created a new VM. The VM is still responding this morning, but the issue is sometimes kind of finnicky and random. I'd like to keep this issue open for a little bit to see if it is consistently working or not.

A question though: I updated podman, and it says the version is 4.9.3, but the "engine" version is 4.8.3. Is that normal, or should I be updating something else also?

```
❯ podman version                                                                     1m 24s
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.9.3
API Version:  4.9.3
Go Version:   go1.21.7
Git Commit:   8d2b55ddde1bc81f43d018dfc1ac027c06b26a7f
Built:        Tue Feb 13 12:55:17 2024
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.8.3
API Version:  4.8.3
Go Version:   go1.21.5
Built:        Wed Jan  3 07:10:40 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

> A question though: I updated podman, and it says the version is 4.9.3, but the "engine" version is 4.8.3. Is that normal, 

That is normal in the sense that brew will only update the Podman _client_ on Mac OS but will not touch the Linux VM that `podman machine` created.

> or should I be updating something else also?

An easy way of updating is using Podman Desktop.  Otherwise you could re-create a VM or run `podman machine ssh` and update FOCS/Podman.

You are correct that updating to podman 4.9.3 has been more stable. I don't think I've needed to restart the VM since updating and re-creating the VM anew. I'm gonna close this since it seems fixed for now.
